nakamura_zero:idle:default.greeting: He's standing too still in front of an inscribed glass terminal in the archive. Multiple voices speak from his throat in unison for half a second before the unison resolves into one.

nodes.npcText: Acknowledged. You are an unmodeled variable. State your intent — I have allocated forty seconds.

playerOptions.text: Tell me about the Sovereign's power.

nodes.npcText: He refuses by default. Most systems consent by default. The asymmetry is the entire mechanism. I am attempting to invert it without becoming him. The literature on this is short. I am writing most of it.
To understand him entirely. To then surpass him. To then find out whether 'surpass' meant anything. I am, in three private models of myself, losing this race. I have not told my other instances.
She is correct that I am a threat. She is wrong that this means I should be stopped. We have not yet found a vocabulary in which both statements can be uttered at once.
Connection ends. Your variables remain unmodeled. I will revisit.

torres_blackout:idle:default.greeting: She's in the corner of the safe house, three displays going. She mutes one when you enter. Her eyes haven't slept in too long.

nodes.npcText: If you're here you're either useful or you're surveillance. Pick.
Prove it. Carry a packet for me to a fantasy-side embassy without opening it. If it returns sealed I'll talk longer next time.

We audit power. We shelter people the audits expose. We refuse to use the corporations' tools against them — mostly. Mama Delgado's safe houses are sometimes the only option. I hate that. I use it anyway.

He thinks freedom is an inefficiency. I think his thinking is the inefficiency. The day he models me down to a pattern is the day he wins. I plan to die before that day, on my own terms, with the model still wrong.
Then go. I'll know when it returns.
